title: A bill to modify the requirements for transfers of United States defense articles and defense services among the Baltic states.
plain_summary: This bill makes it easier for three countries in Europe - Estonia, Lithuania, and Latvia - to share US military equipment and services with each other. Right now, these countries need special permission from the US government every time they want to share this equipment. The new law would let them share freely without asking for permission each time. The bill also tells the Defense Secretary to create a way for these three countries to share special rocket ammunition called HIMARS for their military training and operations.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1 Modification of Requirements for Transfers of United States Defense Articles and Defense Services Among Baltic States
sections.text: This section makes it easier for Baltic countries to share military equipment and services that come from the United States. When the United States gives military equipment or services to Estonia, Lithuania, or Latvia, those countries can now share them with each other without asking the United States for permission first. This changes the current rules that normally require approval before transferring such items. The Secretary of Defense must create a special sharing system among these three countries for ammunition used by High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems, which are called HIMARS. This system will let the Baltic countries share this ammunition for both training exercises and actual military operations. The term Baltic state means Estonia, Lithuania, and Latvia. Defense article and defense service have the same meanings given in section forty seven of the Arms Export Control Act, which is a federal law that controls how military equipment and services are sold or given to other countries.
